name: Spanakopitas
recipeInstructions:
1. In butter or oil, sauté green onion until soft.
2. Squeeze excess moisture from spinach and chop by hand or in a food processor. Combine green onion with spinach and remaining filling ingredients in a bowl. Mix well and set aside.
3. Lay out Filo sheets covered with waxed paper and a damp towel. Take two sheets at a time, keeping the rest covered. Brush melted butter onto top sheet of each pair.
4. Cut each double-sheet into equal strips, about 2-1/2 inches wide. Place 1 teaspoon of filling 1 inch from the end (nearest you) of each strip.
5. Fold the strips back over the filling so the bottom edge meets the left edge, forming a right angle. Continue folding at right angles to make a triangular shape.
6. Place triangles on baking sheets and keep covered until all are ready to bake. Bake in a 350 degree oven for 20 to 25 minutes or until golden and crisp, turning once.
7. If frozen before baking, separate while still frozen, spread on baking sheets, and bake as above, allowing an extra 10 minutes baking time.
